events.text: Ryan Torres, 80-year-old White male, Former smoker (quit 5 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the Urinary bladder. Clinical staging: T3bN0M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C67.9. Stage III muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma of the bladder.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

Underwent surgical resection: Radical cystectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection and ileal conduit urinary diversion. Estimated blood loss 200 mL. Length of stay 6 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after alternative immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after alternative immunotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Ryan Torres
MRN: patient_142ac8704b86
Date of Study: 2023-09-20

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Irregular enhancing mass along the lateral wall of the bladder measuring 5.2 cm with possible extravesical extension.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bladder mass with suspected extravesical extension. Muscle-invasive disease suspected.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Ryan Torres
MRN: patient_142ac8704b86
Specimen ID: S2023-90878
Date of Procedure: 2023-09-22
Date of Report: 2023-09-26

SPECIMEN: Urinary bladder, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.9 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Positive (focal)
GATA3: Positive
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive (focal)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Ryan Torres - Urinary bladder" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.9 x 4.1 x 3.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Ryan Torres
MRN: patient_142ac8704b86
Date: 2023-09-29
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Torres is a 80-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, IIIA. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage III muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma of the bladder. Pathology confirmed high-grade urothelial carcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obstructive sleep apnea

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 2 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of cancer.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild peripheral edema, dry mouth, intermittent headaches, mild cough. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 122/77, HR 81, RR 17, Temp 97.8F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 95.5 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.1, Hgb 13.3, Plt 140, Cr 0.92

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
80-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Ryan Torres
MRN: patient_142ac8704b86
Date of Report: 2023-10-09
Specimen: Urinary bladder (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade urothelial carcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 15.0 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  FGFR3 p.S249C (c.746C>G) - Missense (Exon 7) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 p.R445* - Nonsense (Exon 14)
  TP53 p.R282W - Missense (Exon 8)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ERBB2 p.S310F - VUS
  PIK3CA p.E545A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - FGFR3 S249C: FDA-approved erdafitinib for FGFR-altered advanced urothelial carcinoma.
  - Elevated TMB may support immunotherapy benefit.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 696x coverage.
Tumor content: 33%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Ryan Torres
MRN: patient_142ac8704b86
Date: 2023-11-09
Provider: Dr. Priya Sharma

CHIEF COMPLAINT: cycle 2 assessment

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Torres is a 80-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient has some concerns about side effects. Appetite is decreased but maintaining weight.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild peripheral edema, abdominal bloating, fatigue, joint pain. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 118/76, HR 82, RR 18, Temp 97.5F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 72.9 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.0, Hgb 10.7, Plt 268, Cr 0.83

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
80-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. cycle 2 assessment.

1. High-grade urothelial carcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Monitoring for treatment-related toxicity
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 4 weeks

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Medical Oncology

SPECIMEN: Urinary bladder, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.6 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Positive (focal)
GATA3: Positive
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive (focal)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Ryan Torres - Urinary bladder" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.6 x 3.9 x 2.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Ryan Torres
MRN: patient_142ac8704b86
Date of Study: 2025-03-17

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 9 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Irregular enhancing mass along the lateral wall of the bladder measuring 3.6 cm with likely extravesical extension.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bladder mass with suspected extravesical extension. Muscle-invasive disease suspected.
2. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology
